authorities.name: USDA Forest Service, Pacific Southwest Research Station

sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16, Fishing Seasons and Regulations (Jan. 1, 2026 – Dec. 31, 2027) — Douglas County, "Lake Tahoe and tributaries" listing
sources.quotedText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish. Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

sources.title: CR 25-16 — Douglas County CLOSED WATERS (McFaul Creek not listed as closed; only Glenbrook Creek is)
sources.quotedText: Topaz Lake within the jetties of Topaz Marina. Lake Tahoe within the boat launch area, inside the jetty at the Cave Rock boat ramp, north end. Glenbrook Creek from Lake Tahoe to Highway 50.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: Lake Tahoe Watershed Assessment, Volume I (USDA Forest Service Gen. Tech. Rep. PSW-GTR-175) — Time Line, "McFaul/Burke/Edgewood Creeks Watersheds (Kingsbury)"
sources.quotedText: Time Line of Human Land Use and Environmental Conditions, McFaul/Burke/Edgewood Creeks Watersheds (Kingsbury)

sources.title: List of Lake Tahoe inflow streams — Wikipedia (East shore section; McFaul Creek)
sources.quotedText: flows into Lake Tahoe at Zephyr Cove, Nevada

parameters.note: McFaul Creek is an east-shore Lake Tahoe tributary lying entirely within Douglas County, Nevada; it flows into Lake Tahoe at Zephyr Cove (Wikipedia's 'List of Lake Tahoe inflow streams' places it in the East shore group of streams that flow into the lake; the USFS Lake Tahoe Watershed Assessment groups it as one of the 'McFaul/Burke/Edgewood Creeks Watersheds (Kingsbury)' and records 'atabi samat' — the Washoe name for the creek — meaning 'fish swimming through, fish passage'). It is not named individually in CR 25-16, so the applicable Douglas County listing is 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' — open year around. McFaul Creek is NOT in Douglas County's closed-waters list (only Glenbrook Creek is), so it is open all year. The California Lake Tahoe tributary catch-and-release closure (CCR T14 §7.50(b)(152)) does not reach this creek: that special regulation applies only to Tahoe tributaries in Placer and El Dorado counties, California — not to Nevada-side tributaries, which NDOW governs. No USGS streamgage exists on McFaul Creek, so its mouth position is corroborated by the Wikipedia inflow list and OSM rather than a gage name.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Open year-round (Nevada / Douglas County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing; McFaul Creek is a Lake Tahoe tributary and is not in the county's closed-waters list).
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around.

parameters.counting_rule: Douglas County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' limit: 5 game fish daily, 10 game fish in possession. 'Game fish' is the combined coldwater/warmwater game-fish count; no species sub-limit is listed for this row in CR 25-16.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: 5 game fish per day, 10 in possession (Nevada Douglas County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' limit).
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish.

parameters.note: The 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' row carries restricted hours (1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset) — narrower than Douglas County's general 'All waters' rule of any hour of the day or night.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing permitted from 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

parameters.note: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers 12 and older. McFaul Creek is a Nevada tributary (Douglas Co.), not the open water of Lake Tahoe; the Lake Tahoe CA/NV reciprocal-license arrangement is written for fishing anywhere ON the lake, so a Nevada license (not a California license alone) should be carried on the creek.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; none required under 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
